source_pr.title: parser:support order by in select from dual
source_pr.body:
Thank you for working on TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.

## What have you changed? (mandatory)

Please explain **IN DETAIL** what the changes are in this PR and why they are needed:

Just for compatibly the mysql server's behaviour

## What are the type of the changes (mandatory)?

A improvement for the issue:

https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/6782

## How has this PR been tested (mandatory)?

Passed make dev test.

## Does this PR affect documentation (docs/docs-cn) update? (optional)

nope

## Refer to a related PR or issue link (optional)

## Benchmark result if necessary (optional)

## Add a few positive/negative examples (optional)

symptoms: {"select 1 as a from dual order by a", true}, (parser/parser_test.go)
failure_signature: {"select 1 as a from dual order by a", true}, (parser/parser_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: {"select 1 as a from dual order by a", true}, (parser/parser_test.go)
